items.title: Integrated immunophenotypic and ProMisE molecular profiling as predictors of immune checkpoint inhibitor response in recurrent endometrial cancer
items.description: CONCLUSIONS: Tumor immune phenotypes provide clinically relevant information complementary to ProMisE classification. Rather than indicating a single universal determinant, our results support a model in which cytotoxic T-cell infiltration and immunosuppressive pathways jointly shape ICI outcome in recurrent EC. Integrating ProMisE classification with immunological profiling may improve patient stratification of ICI benefits.
items.conclusion: Tumor immune phenotypes provide clinically relevant information complementary to ProMisE classification. items.description: Existential isolation (EI) has been linked to depressive symptoms, yet evidence suggests it is not always distressing and can be meaningful for some individuals by satisfying their desire for uniqueness. items.description: Intermolecular gold(I)-catalyzed dimerization of internal alkynes remains a significant challenge. items.title: Contralateral Breast Background Parenchymal Enhancement: A Physiologic Marker, not a Predictor of ER Status
items.description: CONCLUSION: While BPE reflects hormonal influences, its role in predicting tumor ER expression in early-stage cancer is limited. However, it provides a reproducible method for assessing breast tissue physiology, with potential utility in evaluating treatment response and hormonal effects. Furthermore, the study also delineates the need for larger, multicenter investigations to explore BPE's potential as a complementary biomarker for breast cancer characterization and treatment response.
items.conclusion: While BPE reflects hormonal influences, its role in predicting tumor ER expression in early-stage cancer is limited. However, it provides a reproducible method for assessing breast tissue physiology, with potential utility in evaluating treatment response and hormonal effects

items.description: Male sexual dysfunction remains a complex clinical challenge with limited therapeutic options. Current treatments typically target individual components such as erectile function, sexual motivation, or fertility, but an integrated approach is lacking. items.description: Breast Cancer necessitates effective patient education. items.title: Injury to the thorax is a dominant contributor to post-traumatic neutrophil mediated inflammatory response
items.description: CONCLUSION: Injuries to internal thoracic organs elicit a more pronounced post-traumatic neutrophil associated inflammatory response than injuries of similar severity in other body regions. This might be due to greater release of DAMPs from the soft tissue of the lungs. Understanding the body region-specific activation of neutrophil associated inflammation may support more patient-tailored surgical strategies to lower the infection risk.
items.conclusion: Injuries to internal thoracic organs elicit a more pronounced post-traumatic neutrophil associated inflammatory response than injuries of similar severity in other body regions. This might be due to greater release of DAMPs from the soft tissue of the lungs

items.description: CONCLUSION: While some practices align with existing recommendations, other aspects require further discussion. The age at which patients should undergo unilateral CA repair still needs clarification. Preoperative hearing tests were not commonly implemented. The use of stents and perioperative antibiotics should be discussed further.

items.description: CONCLUSIONS AND PRACTICAL IMPLICATIONS: The clinical usefulness of light-based adjuncts cannot be determined with a high degree of certainty for patients with or without mucosal abnormalities.
